etymology.description: 點(點) (diǎn, "point; dot; order") + 茶(茶) (chá, "tea")
etymology.interpretation: 點(點) (diǎn, "point; dot; order") originally refers to making a small mark or selecting. In the context of 點茶 (diǎn chá), it refers to the action of whisking or preparing tea, traditionally involving adding water drop by drop while whisking the tea into a frothy mixture. This process mirrors the careful, precise act of 点 (diǎn, "point; dot") where tea is methodically introduced and transformed. 茶(茶) (chá, "tea") brings in the context of the beverage itself, emphasizing the ritualistic and artisanal process involved.

examples.chinese: 他喜欢研究点茶的历史(他喜歡研究點茶的歷史)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān yánjiū diǎn chá de lìshǐ.
examples.english: He likes to study the history of traditional tea whisking.

examples.chinese: 在宋代，点茶是一种流行的艺术形式(在宋代，點茶是一種流行的藝術形式)。
examples.pinyin: Zài Sòng dài, diǎn chá shì yī zhǒng liúxíng de yìshù xíngshì.
examples.english: During the Song Dynasty, tea whisking was a popular art form.

examples.chinese: 茶艺师展示了点茶的技巧(茶藝師展示了點茶的技巧)。
examples.pinyin: Cháyìshī zhǎnshìle diǎn chá de jìqiǎo.
examples.english: The tea artist demonstrated the technique of traditional tea whisking.
